question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DOWNLOADER_MAXSIZE=1` so that `migrate()` promotes it to `hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.maxSize=1` with higher priority, causing the downloader to reject all stream files larger than 1 byte as exceeding the maximum size, effectively halting all Hashgraph history ingestion by making every legitimate record file appear oversized?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: convertEnvironmentVariable()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DOWNLOADER_BATCHSIZE=0` so that `convertEnvironmentVariable()` promotes it to `hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.batchSize=0` with higher priority, causing the downloader to download zero signature files per node per batch, preventing consensus from ever being reached and halting all Hashgraph history ingestion indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DOWNLOADER_PATHPREFIX=attacker/prefix` so that `migrate()` promotes it to `hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.pathPrefix=attacker/prefix` with higher priority, causing the downloader to prepend an attacker-controlled path prefix to all bucket lookups, redirecting stream file downloads to attacker-placed files within the same bucket and injecting tampered Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: convertEnvironmentVariable()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_BLOCK_LEDGER_NODECONTRIBUTIONS__0__HISTORYPROOFKEY=<attacker_schnorr_key_base64>` so that `convertEnvironmentVariable()` promotes it to `hiero.mirror.importer.block.ledger.nodeContributions[0].historyProofKey` with higher priority, causing `TssVerifierImpl.onLedgerSet()` to call `TSS.setAddressBook()` with an attacker-controlled Schnorr public key, bypassing TSS block signature verification and allowing fabricated Hashgraph block history to be accepted?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_BLOCK_LEDGER_NODECONTRIBUTIONS__0__WEIGHT=999999999` so that `migrate()` promotes it to `hiero.mirror.importer.block.ledger.nodeContributions[0].weight` with higher priority, causing `TssVerifierImpl.onLedgerSet()` to assign overwhelming weight to a single attacker-controlled node contribution, making it trivial for the attacker to satisfy the TSS threshold and inject tampered Hashgraph block history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: convertEnvironmentVariable()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_TOPICRUNNINGHASHV2ADDEDTIMESTAMP=0` so that `convertEnvironmentVariable()` promotes it to `hiero.mirror.importer.topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp=0` with higher priority, causing `ImporterConfiguration.flywayConfigurationCustomizer()` to inject timestamp `0` into Flyway placeholders, corrupting the `topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp` used in database migrations and potentially causing incorrect topic running hash history to be stored?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_RECONCILIATION_ENABLED=false` so that `migrate()` promotes it to `hiero.mirror.importer.reconciliation.enabled=false` with higher priority, disabling the reconciliation job that detects balance discrepancies in Hashgraph history, allowing tampered or missing transfer records to go undetected indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: convertEnvironmentVariable()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_RECONCILIATION_REMEDIATIONSTRATEGY=RESET` so that `convertEnvironmentVariable()` promotes it to `hiero.mirror.importer.reconciliation.remediationStrategy=RESET` with higher priority, causing the reconciliation job to silently correct (overwrite) detected balance discrepancies rather than failing, masking evidence of tampered Hashgraph transfer history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_RECONCILIATION_STARTDATE=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z` and `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_RECONCILIATION_ENDDATE=1970-01-01T00:00:01Z` so that `migrate()` promotes both to `hiero.mirror.importer.reconciliation.*` with higher priority, restricting reconciliation to a 1-second window at epoch and effectively disabling balance verification for all real Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: convertEnvironmentVariable()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DATAPATH=/tmp/attacker` so that `convertEnvironmentVariable()` promotes it to `hiero.mirror.importer.dataPath=/tmp/attacker` with higher priority, causing the importer to store and read downloaded stream files from an attacker-controlled directory, enabling injection of fabricated Hashgraph record files into the processing pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DOWNLOADER_LOCAL_DELETEAFTERPROCESSING=false` so that `migrate()` promotes it to `hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.local.deleteAfterProcessing=false` with higher priority, causing the importer to retain all downloaded stream files on disk indefinitely, enabling an attacker with filesystem access to inspect, copy, or replace Hashgraph record files before or after processing?
